member.office_address: 2332 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3213

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please use this form to submit Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) appropriations program or language requests to Rep. Adriano Espaillat's office. If you have multiple requests, please submit a separate form for each request. Please provide as much of the requested information as possible to ensure we can fully evaluate your request. DO NOT USE THIS FORM FOR COMMUNITY PROJECT FUNDING REQUESTS. All program and language requests must be submitted through this form in order to be considered. Please don't hesitate to reach out to our office with any questions. You may contact my team at Nia.Thomas@mail.house.gov and Joseph.Newman@mail.house.gov

fields.label: E-mail address
Appropriations Bill / Subcommittee of Jurisdiction
options: AG: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
CJS: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
EW: Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
FSGG: Financial Services and General Government
INT: Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies
LHHS: Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
MILCON: Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
NSRP: National Security, Department of State, and Related Agencies
THUD: Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: CJS: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA and its Science Mission Directorate are funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Federal Agency or Sub-Agency (See here for an index of agencies: https://www.usa.gov/federal-agencies)
fields.rationale: NASA is the primary federal agency for this request.

fields.label: Federal Account - See here for an index of accounts: https://espaillat.house.gov/sites/evo-subsites/espaillat.house.gov/files/evo-media-document/espaillat-agency-accounts-1.pdf
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate is the specific account requested within NASA.

fields.label: Please select whether this is a Program (funding) or Language request.
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific programmatic funding level ($9 billion) for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Funding choice
options: Support the enacted funding level of $ (see below)
Support President Trump's FY2026 Budget request.
fields.rationale: The requested $9 billion for FY2027 represents a $1.75 billion (24%) increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.25 billion, aimed at restoring NASA's scientific capabilities and maintaining leadership against international competition.

fields.label: FY27 Request Amount (required if choice made above says "see below")
fields.rationale: The requested amount for NASA's Science Mission Directorate is $9 billion as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Requested Amount (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request amount is currently unknown as it has not yet been released.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Amount (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The FY2026 enacted amount is $7.250 billion per H.R. 6938 as provided in the reference data.

fields.label: Program Description: Please provide a brief description of the program and justification for federal support at the funding level you are requesting:
fields.draft_value: * NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) executes National Academies' decadal priorities, investing $75,207,386 in NY-13 since FY2023 through 200+ grants and contracts. * This $9B request sustains critical district research at Columbia University ($53.5M in grants) for Earth climate modeling and dark matter detection. * Federal support restores stability after FY2026 disruptions, ensuring a 3-to-1 economic return and securing U.S. leadership against rapid international competition.
fields.rationale: Justification uses the $9 billion ask, SMD account description, economic ROI talking points, and references the FY 2026 disruption as per the advocacy guide.

fields.label: Additional Information: If you would like to include more specific information about this request's impact on communities in New York's 13th Congressional District, please do so here:
fields.draft_value: * $75.2 million in NASA Science obligations (FY23-FY25) supporting NY-13 innovation, including $53.5 million in grants for Columbia University research. * Critical mission support from local firms Scispace LLC ($17.6M cumulative award total) and Trinnovim LLC ($17M cumulative award total). * Transformative district research in climate modeling, dark matter detection, and cosmic mapping, maintaining NY-13's leadership in space science.
fields.rationale: Cites $75.2M total district obligations and identifies Columbia University research (climate change, dark matter, instrumentation) and top contract recipients (Scispace, Trinnovim) from NASA spending data.

fields.label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
fields.draft_value: Yes, Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading a letter in the House.
fields.rationale: The reference context specifically identifies an existing House letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu.

fields.label: Text of proposed language:
fields.draft_value: - Fund NASA's Science Mission Directorate at $9,000,000,000, a $1.750 billion (24%) increase over FY2026 to maintain U.S. leadership against Chinese space ambitions. - Support $75,207,386.32 in recent NY-13 NASA funding, including $53,520,502.45 in grants to Columbia University for climate and astrophysics research. - Secure a 3:1 economic return and stable STEM workforce by adhering to peer-reviewed priorities from the National Academies Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: The reference context indicates N/A for language, but since the field is required for a programmatic request, a standard programmatic line item for the Science account is provided.

fields.label: Please provide a brief description of and justification for your request.
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for NASA SMD to maintain U.S. leadership against international competition and execute Decadal Survey priorities that ensure transformative scientific discovery.
* Sustains NY-13's $75.2 million NASA Science ecosystem, including a $10.7 million Columbia University award for modeling Earth climate change and planetary habitability.
* Protects the specialized national workforce while delivering high economic returns (roughly $3 for every $1 invested) at less than one-third of one percent of federal spending.
fields.rationale: Uses advocacy talking points on China competition and Decadal Survey priorities, while linking the workforce benefit to Columbia University's presence in NY-13.

fields.label: OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
fields.draft_value: * $75.2M in recent NASA Science funding for NY-13, including $53.5M in grants to Columbia University for research such as $10.8M for Earth climate change and planetary habitability. * Over $17.6M in local contracts to Scispace LLC, leveraging NASA's $3-to-$1 economic return to sustain a high-tech workforce and maintain competitive advantage over China's space ambitions. * $9B funding level to stabilize the aerospace talent pipeline and advance non-partisan Decadal Survey priorities supported by the American Astronomical Society and American Geophysical Union.
fields.rationale: Integrates economic ROI data, national security themes, and mentions specific allied organizations (AAS, AGU) provided in the asks configuration.

fields.label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
fields.draft_value: * Yes. Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading a multi-member programmatic support letter in the House.
fields.rationale: Directly cites the congressional support information for the existing House letter.

sections.description: Note: bill language requests are rare and more difficult to fulfill.

fields.label: Text of proposed language:
fields.draft_value: - $9,000,000,000 for NASA Science to maintain U.S. leadership against Chinese expansion and restore stability following FY2026 funding cuts. - Sustains $75,207,386.32 in recent local investment across NY-13, supporting a specialized workforce and driving a 3-to-1 economic return on every dollar. - Supports critical research at Columbia University, a top recipient of $53,520,502.45 in NASA grants for Earth climate change modeling and dark matter discovery.
fields.rationale: Identical to previous language field; provided for consistency in required form sections.

fields.label: Please provide a brief description of and justification for your request.
fields.draft_value:
* This $9 billion request secures U.S. leadership against international competition for less than 1/3 of 1% of federal spending, yielding an estimated $3 economic return for every $1 invested.
* Funding sustains $75.2 million in recent NASA Science spending within NY-13, including $53.5 million in grants to Columbia University for critical Earth climate modeling and dark matter research.
* Restores stability to the specialized scientific workforce and industrial base, ensuring NASA can execute the nonpartisan, high-priority missions identified in National Academies Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: Identical to previous justification field; provided for consistency in required form sections.

fields.label: OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science invested $75,207,386.32 in NY-13 from FY2023-FY2025, including $53,520,502.45 in grants to Columbia University for climate change modeling and dark matter research.
* This $9 billion request restores stability to a workforce that returns $3 to the economy for every $1 invested, ensuring the U.S. maintains its technological edge over China's rapidly expanding space ambitions.
* Supported by the American Astronomical Society and American Geophysical Union, this funding upholds Title 51 responsibilities and preserves the STEM talent pipeline across New York institutions.
fields.rationale: Identical to previous optional info field; provided for consistency in required form sections.

fields.label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
fields.draft_value: * Yes. Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading a multi-member programmatic support letter in the House.
fields.rationale: Identical to previous letter lead field; provided for consistency in required form sections.

details.field_label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
Please provide a brief description of and justification for your request.
details.original_value:
* $9 billion for NASA SMD ensures the U.S. remains the world leader in space science, responding to aggressive expansion by international competitors.
* Provides critical funding for missions identified by Decadal Surveys as essential for transformative discovery and national prestige.
* Protects the specialized workforce and industrial base in New York and nationwide that are vital for future technological innovation.
* Supports district institutions like Columbia University in advancing our understanding of Earth's climate and the wider solar system.
critique_issues: The response uses four bullet points, exceeding the limit of 1-3 concise bullets required by the formatting criteria.

While the draft mentions Columbia University, it lacks the specific data points provided in the fact sheet, such as the $75.2 million in total NASA Science spending within NY-13 or the $53.5 million in grants awarded to Columbia University for research like climate modeling.

The justification is slightly generic; incorporating the 'one-third of one percent of federal spending' or the 'three-to-one economic return' talking points would make the argument more compelling as per the advocacy guidelines.

details.field_label: Please provide a brief description of and justification for your request.
details.original_value:
* $9 billion for NASA SMD ensures the U.S. remains the world leader in space science, responding to aggressive expansion by international competitors.
* Provides critical funding for missions identified by Decadal Surveys as essential for transformative discovery and national prestige.
* Protects the specialized workforce and industrial base in New York and nationwide that are vital for future technological innovation.
* Supports district institutions like Columbia University in advancing our understanding of Earth's climate and the wider solar system.
critique_issues: The response uses 4 bullet points, which exceeds the formatting requirement of 1-3 concise bullets for textarea fields.

The justification would be more compelling and data-rich by citing specific local funding figures, such as the $75.2 million in total NASA Science spending in NY-13 or the $10.7 million award to Columbia University for climate research.

details.field_label: Program Description: Please provide a brief description of the program and justification for federal support at the funding level you are requesting:
details.original_value:
* NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) conducts decadal-driven research in Earth science, planetary exploration, astrophysics, and heliophysics.
* $9 billion request restores program stability following FY 2026 disruptions and workforce layoffs, ensuring continued U.S. leadership in space.
* Direct investment in the national innovation base provides a 3-to-1 economic return and sustains critical high-tech manufacturing and research capabilities.
* Scientific roadmaps produced by the National Academies provide a rigorous, nonpartisan basis for this transformative discovery-driven investment.
critique_issues: The response exceeds the maximum limit of 3 bullet points.

The draft lacks specific local data citations provided in the context, such as the $75.2 million in NASA Science spending in NY-13 or specific district recipients like Columbia University ($53.5M in grants) to ground the justification.

The description of the program is somewhat generic; citing specific outcomes or mission names from the 'Detailed Spending Data' (e.g., Earth climate modeling or dark matter research) would better satisfy the specificity criterion.

details.field_label: Additional Information: If you would like to include more specific information about this request's impact on communities in New York's 13th Congressional District, please do so here:
details.original_value:
* Institutions in NY-13 received $75.2 million in NASA Science obligations between FY 2023 and FY 2025, supporting a robust local research economy.
* Columbia University is a primary recipient, conducting critical work on Earth climate modeling, dark matter research, and cosmic mapping instrumentation.
* Local firms like Scispace LLC ($17.6M) and Trinnovim LLC ($17M) provide essential technical support for national space missions.
* District research includes developing a trusted data archive for space samples, maintaining NY-13's position at the forefront of scientific discovery.
critique_issues: The draft uses four bullet points, exceeding the limit of 1-3 concise bullet points specified in the formatting criteria.
When citing specific award amounts for Scispace LLC and Trinnovim LLC, the draft does not explicitly note that these are cumulative award totals as required by the data precision guidelines.

details.field_label: OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
details.original_value:
* Space science returns $3 to the economy for every $1 invested, strengthening the STEM talent pipeline and commercial spinoff potential.
* National security and economic competitiveness are bolstered by advancing technological frontiers that no private entity can reach alone.
* Broad support from the American Astronomical Society (AAS) and American Geophysical Union (AGU) underscores the request's scientific consensus.
* Public polling shows space science remains a top priority and a point of non-partisan national pride for the American public.
critique_issues: The draft fails the specificity criterion by omitting all local data--such as the $75,207,386 in recent NASA spending in NY-13 or the $53.5M in grants to the Trustees of Columbia University--despite the field explicitly asking for impact to the 13th District.
The response exceeds the formatting limit of 1-3 bullet points (it uses four).
To improve, consolidate the general talking points and include specific citations such as Scispace LLC ($17.6M in contracts) or the $10.8M Columbia University grant for climate change research.
FORMAT: The response must use 1-3 concise bullet points rather than a single line of text.
ARGUMENT: The response is not compelling; it lacks the advocacy-driven narrative fragments requested in the guidelines to justify the $9 billion funding level.
SPECIFICITY: Include district-specific data to strengthen the case, such as the $75,207,386.32 in recent NASA Science spending in NY-13.
SPECIFICITY: Reference local impact by citing major recipients like The Trustees of Columbia University, which received over $53 million in NASA grants during the FY2023-FY2025 period.

details.field_label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.

OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
details.original_value:
* Space science returns $3 to the economy for every $1 invested, strengthening the STEM talent pipeline and commercial spinoff potential.
* National security and economic competitiveness are bolstered by advancing technological frontiers that no private entity can reach alone.
* Broad support from the American Astronomical Society (AAS) and American Geophysical Union (AGU) underscores the request's scientific consensus.
* Public polling shows space science remains a top priority and a point of non-partisan national pride for the American public.
critique_issues: The draft exceeds the 3-bullet limit for textarea fields.

The response fails to include specific data for NY-13 despite the field label explicitly asking for impact to the 13th district; it should cite the $75.2 million in total local spending or the $53.5 million in grants to Columbia University.
The draft relies on generic talking points and fails the specificity criterion by not including local recipient names or specific dollar amounts from the provided context.
The response must use a 1-3 concise bullet point format rather than a single line of text.
The draft fails the specificity criterion by not citing local data, such as the $75,207,386.32 in NASA spending within NY-13 or the $53,520,502.45 in grants to The Trustees of Columbia University.

The response does not make a persuasive case or include talking points, such as the 24% increase over FY2026 enacted levels or the importance of U.S. leadership in the context of competition with China.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: 23-nup2023-0090  interdisciplinary research on Earth and planetary system modeling  the impacts of climate change  and the habitability of other worlds 2023-2026
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling Earth climate change and planetary habitability research.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The gaps experiment: a search for dark matter using low-energy antiparticles
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Searching for dark matter using low-energy antiparticles.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced balloon instrumentation for cosmic background mapping.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The department of applied mathematics and physics (APAM) at Columbia University (CU) proposes to renew cooperative agreement nnx15aj05a with the NASA goddard institute for space studies. The scope of work spans three broad themes. Earth system modeling will involve the development and evaluation of atmospheric  oceanic  land surface and cryospheric model components to better understand past natural and anthropogenic changes in climate and  as a central part of the us contribution to internationallycoordinated efforts to make projections of possible future climates. Research in comparative planetary climatologies will extend the Earth system modeling effort to better understand the conditions under which other planets in our solar system have been habitable  and how we might detect habitable planets at greater distances. Remote sensing research will include fundamental work in radiative transfer  and significant involvement in major field campaigns to better measure aerosols  clouds  and ocean surface properties. The cooperative agreement is the administrative mechanism through which the unique expertise of NASA and apam can be leveraged to advance our understanding of the Earth system  other planets  and cutting edge measurements  building upon the rich scientific history of both institutions.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced climate models for Earth and other planets.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The astromaterials data system: enabling fair astromaterials samples data through trusted archiving services and broad community engagement
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Building a trusted data archive for NASA's space samples.
